questions.question: 中文导读：What is Promptable Concept Segmentation (PCS), introduced by SAM 3?
options: 中文选项：Segmenting all instances of a concept described by a short noun phrase or image exemplar in a single forward pass, returning masks plus unique instance IDs
中文选项：A visual prompt (point/box) segmentation like classic SAM
中文选项：A multi-step chain of a detector followed by SAM
中文选项：A tracking-only mode
questions.explanation: 中文解析：PCS is SAM 3's signature capability. You pass 'yellow school bus' or an image exemplar of a bus; SAM 3 returns every matching instance with its own mask and unique ID, plus a presence score. Classic SAM needed one prompt per instance; PCS produces all matches end-to-end.

questions.question: 中文导读：Why keep a decoupled detector + SAM 2 pipeline (Grounded SAM 2) in 2026 if SAM 3 already does text-prompted segmentation?
options: 中文选项：Grounded SAM 2 is always more accurate

中文选项：Modularity — you can swap in different open-vocabulary detectors (DINO-X, Florence-2, Grounding DINO 1.5) for different domains, license constraints, or threshold behaviour; SAM 3's architecture is monolithic
中文选项：SAM 3 does not support video
中文选项：SAM 3 is only available in the cloud
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Grounded SAM 2 is a composition of a detector and SAM 2 with frozen weights. That modularity is sometimes exactly what you need — a medical-imaging detector, a license-friendly detector, or tight threshold control. SAM 3 is more accurate end-to-end for common use but harder to customise. Both have production roles in 2026.

questions.question: 中文导读：SAM 3's presence head produces what?
options: 中文选项：The list of candidate bounding boxes
中文选项：A tracking memory bank
中文选项：A scalar probability that the queried concept exists in the image, decoupled from localisation; lets the model say 'not present' cleanly and reduces false positives on absent concepts
中文选项：The final mask
questions.explanation: 中文解析：The presence head separates the 'is this here?' decision from the 'where is it?' decision. A model that must produce boxes whenever asked tends to hallucinate matches for absent concepts. With a presence head, SAM 3 can return zero detections cleanly. This also improves discrimination between closely related prompts (e.g., 'a player in white' vs 'a player in red').

questions.question: 中文导读：SAM 3.1 Object Multiplex (March 2026) introduced a shared-memory mechanism for tracking. What does it replace?
options: 中文选项：SAM 2 altogether
中文选项：Per-instance separate memory banks; Multiplex collapses them into one shared memory with per-instance queries so tracking N objects runs substantially faster while keeping accuracy
中文选项：The whole presence head
中文选项：The ViT backbone
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Prior SAM 2 / SAM 3 tracking maintained one memory bank per tracked instance, so cost grew linearly with object count. Object Multiplex introduces a single shared memory plus per-instance queries that fetch instance-specific features. Many-instance tracking is now efficient — essential for crowds and dense multi-object scenes.

questions.question: 中文导读：You need real-time open-vocabulary DETECTION (boxes only, no masks) on an edge device. Which 2026 model family is the right choice?
options: 中文选项：SAM 3
中文选项：A custom CLIP + detector chain
中文选项：Grounded SAM 2
中文选项：YOLO-World (and related real-time open-vocab detectors); SAM 3 produces masks and is heavier, while YOLO-World is designed specifically for boxes at high fps on edge hardware
questions.explanation: 中文解析：YOLO-World and similar models (OV-DINO, LLMDet) are real-time open-vocabulary detectors. They do not produce masks but they hit 30-60 fps at 640x640 on modest GPUs. SAM 3 is the right choice when masks and tracking matter. Always match tool weight to the task: detection-only is fine without mask overhead.
